title: Kadampanad Church
tagline: Kadampanad Church — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Kadampanad Church is a much-loved spiritual town in Kerala, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Kadampanad, popularly known as Kadampanad Church ( കടമ്പനാട് പള്ളി ) is an ancient church of Saint Thomas Christians situated in the Central Travancore village of Kadampanad, in Pathanamthitta District, Kerala, India. This Church belongs to the Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church under the Diocese of Adoor-Kadampanad.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kadampanad Bhagavathy Temple
topPlaces.description: Kadampanad Bhagavathy Temple is a Hindu temple situated at Kadampanad in Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India. It is 12 km from Adoor, 6.6 km from Mannady and 10 km from Sasthamkotta.

topPlaces.name: Kadampanad
topPlaces.description: Kadampanad grama panchayath is the southernmost part of Pathanamthitta district. It comes under Parakkode block of Adoor taluk.

topPlaces.name: Iverkala
topPlaces.description: Iverkala is a rural village locality in Kollam district in the Indian state of Kerala. Situated near the banks of the Kallada River, the region is known for its agricultural landscape, riverine geography, temples, and local folklore associated with the Mahabharata.

topPlaces.name: Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple
topPlaces.description: Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada, popularly known as Peruviruthy Malanada or Malanada is a Mala Appooppan shrine in South India. (It is believed that the 'Sankalpa Moorthy' at Malanada is 'Duryodhanan').

topPlaces.name: Malanada firework disaster
topPlaces.description: The Malanada firework disaster was a disaster in which 33 people lost their lives after a fire broke out in the Malanada temple at Poruvazhy in Kollam district, Kerala, India, on 23 March 1990. An electric spark in the place where the fireworks where stored caused the explosion.

topPlaces.name: Kunnathur, Kerala
topPlaces.description: Kunnathur (Kunnattūr,കുന്നത്തൂർ) is a taluk in Kollam district in the Indian state of Kerala. The capital of Kunnathur taluk is Sasthamkotta.

topPlaces.name: Erathu
topPlaces.description: Erathu is a gram panchayat in Pathanamthitta district, Adoor Tehsil, Parakod Block in the state of Kerala, India. This panchayat covers the revenue entity of Erathu village and has an area of 21.74 km2.

topPlaces.name: Faith Theological Seminary, Manakala
topPlaces.description: Faith Theological Seminary, Manakala (FTS Manakala) is a Pentecostal theological seminary located in Manakala, Pathanamthitta, Kerala, India. It was founded in 1970 and is affiliated with the Senate of Serampore College (University).

items.note: Kadampanad Bhagavathy Temple is a Hindu temple situated at Kadampanad in Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India. It is 12 km from Adoor, 6.6 km from Mannady and 10 km from Sasthamkotta.
Kadampanad grama panchayath is the southernmost part of Pathanamthitta district. It comes under Parakkode block of Adoor taluk.

Iverkala is a rural village locality in Kollam district in the Indian state of Kerala. Situated near the banks of the Kallada River, the region is known for its agricultural landscape, riverine geography, temples, and local folklore associated with the Mahabharata.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple & around

items.note: Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada, popularly known as Peruviruthy Malanada or Malanada is a Mala Appooppan shrine in South India. (It is believed that the 'Sankalpa Moorthy' at Malanada is 'Duryodhanan').

The Malanada firework disaster was a disaster in which 33 people lost their lives after a fire broke out in the Malanada temple at Poruvazhy in Kollam district, Kerala, India, on 23 March 1990. An electric spark in the place where the fireworks where stored caused the explosion.
Kunnathur (Kunnattūr,കുന്നത്തൂർ) is a taluk in Kollam district in the Indian state of Kerala. The capital of Kunnathur taluk is Sasthamkotta.

Erathu is a gram panchayat in Pathanamthitta district, Adoor Tehsil, Parakod Block in the state of Kerala, India. This panchayat covers the revenue entity of Erathu village and has an area of 21.74 km2.

Faith Theological Seminary, Manakala (FTS Manakala) is a Pentecostal theological seminary located in Manakala, Pathanamthitta, Kerala, India. It was founded in 1970 and is affiliated with the Senate of Serampore College (University).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kadampanad Church?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kadampanad Church is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kadampanad Church?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (73 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (73 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kadampanad Church?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kadampanad Church at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kadampanad Church?
faq.a: Stays in Kadampanad Church start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kadampanad Church?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kadampanad Bhagavathy Temple, Kadampanad, Iverkala, Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple, Malanada firework disaster.
